text.title: claude-messages-api Core Rules
text.summary: 7 testable rules for claude-messages-api. Each rule has statement + rationale + source.

rule.statement: Validate `response.stop_reason` BEFORE accessing `response.content[0].text`; type-check each block since `content` is `list[TextBlock|ToolUseBlock|ThinkingBlock]`.
rule.rationale: Indexing blindly into `content[0].text` raises AttributeError when the first block is a tool_use or thinking block.

rule.statement: Append the FULL `response.content` list (not just text) as the assistant turn in multi-turn history; consecutive same-role messages raise 400.
rule.rationale: Missing tool_use or thinking blocks corrupt conversation state and trigger 400 on the next turn.

rule.statement: Always set `max_tokens` explicitly; treat `stop_reason=="max_tokens"` as silent truncation: branch on it (do not parse), retry with larger budget OR decompose the task.
rule.rationale: Default max_tokens is low; truncation is not signalled by HTTP status; downstream parsers handle it as a parse error not a truncation.

rule.statement: For multimodal user content: place image / PDF parts BEFORE the text part in the `content` array; base64-encode local files; pre-fetch URL images before production calls.
rule.rationale: Image-first ordering empirically improves comprehension; URL-based images can fail with content-error blocks at inference time.

rule.statement: PDFs: validate page count ≤100 and size ≤32MB BEFORE base64-encoding; JPEG/PNG/WebP/GIF: validate ≤20MB.
rule.rationale: Over-limit assets fail at inference time after encoding+transmission, wasting bandwidth; the failure is an API error, not a truncation.

rule.statement: For streaming + tool use: accumulate `input_json_delta.partial_json` per content block, parse only after `content_block_stop`; never inline-parse partial JSON.
rule.rationale: Partial JSON is not valid; inline parsing raises JSONDecodeError; Claude SSE differs from OpenAI SSE so blind cross-provider parsing fails.

rule.statement: Tag every call with `metadata={"user_id":"..."}` for abuse detection and per-user cost attribution.
rule.rationale: Without metadata, Anthropic's abuse signals and your own cost dashboard cannot map calls back to a user.
